rationale: The main alert (A0) shows `cmd.exe` being used to add a new user, '[id]_CMD', with a password. This is immediately followed by EV-001 creating another user, '[id]_PowerShell', via PowerShell, and EV-002 adding a third user, '[id]_Admin', to the local administrators group. This sequence of creating multiple new users and elevating privileges is highly indicative of malicious activity, specifically privilege escalation and persistence attempts. While subsequent events (EV-003, EV-004, EV-005) show attempts to delete these users, this could be an attempt to cover tracks after successful compromise or part of a testing framework, but the initial creation and privilege escalation are unequivocally malicious.
